name: Sentier Dessin du Kazé — loop from Saint-Élix
name_original: Sentier Dessin du Kazé — boucle depuis Saint-Élix
region_intro: Hiking around Saint-Élix offers varied landscapes, characterized by gentle slopes, river valleys, and a diverse hydrographic network including the Louge River and smaller streams. The region provides opportunities to explore natural settings and historical heritage, with areas like Forêt de Bouconne nearby. These natural features contribute to a range of outdoor activities suitable for different preferences. Best hiking trails around Saint-Élix The most popular hiking route is The trail to p'Art — loop from Simorre, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sentier Dessin du Kazé — loop from Saint-Élix, an easy 2.3 miles (3.7 km) path. This trail features a sculpture by a Japanese artist, representing the wind. Local hikers also love the Simorre – Saint Cérase Church loop from Simorre, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Saint-Élix is defined by gentle slopes, river valleys, and natural settings. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more moderate hikes. The routes in Saint-Élix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 270 hikers have used komoot to explore Saint-Élix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Saint-Élix?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Saint-Élix, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has recorded 26 routes in total for the area.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes in Saint-Élix?
A: The hiking trails around Saint-Élix are predominantly moderate, with 19 routes falling into this category. There are also 6 easy trails perfect for a relaxed stroll, and 1 more challenging route for those seeking a greater adventure.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in the Saint-Élix area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Saint-Élix are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, you can explore the local countryside on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187665653/the-trail-to-p-art-loop-from-simorre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The trail to p'Art — loop from Simorre&lt;/a&gt;, or discover unique artwork on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186699893/sentier-dessin-du-kaze-loop-from-saint-elix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentier Dessin du Kazé — loop from Saint-Élix&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Saint-Élix?
A: The region is characterized by gentle slopes, river valleys, and a diverse hydrographic network. You'll encounter watercourses like the Louge River and smaller streams. For more dramatic natural landscapes, the Gorges de la Save are about 39 km away, and the Forêt de Bouconne, approximately 41 km away, offers beautiful woodlands.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Saint-Élix?
A: Yes, the area is well-suited for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186699893/sentier-dessin-du-kaze-loop-from-saint-elix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentier Dessin du Kazé — loop from Saint-Élix&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.3-mile path that is great for all ages. The 'boucle des 3 châteaux' (loop of the 3 castles) mentioned in regional research is also considered family-friendly, partly running along a canal and stream.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest along the trails?
A: You can discover several interesting points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186699893/sentier-dessin-du-kaze-loop-from-saint-elix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentier Dessin du Kazé — loop from Saint-Élix&lt;/a&gt; features the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2994309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kazé - artwork by Japanese artist Teruhisa Suzuki&lt;/a&gt;. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6981837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Simorre – Saint Cérase Church loop from Simorre&lt;/a&gt;, passes by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3671616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Cérase Church&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Saint-Élix?
A: The routes in Saint-Élix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.75 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, gentle slopes, and the opportunity to explore both natural settings and historical heritage.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature unique art installations?
A: Yes, the region offers trails with unique artistic elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186699893/sentier-dessin-du-kaze-loop-from-saint-elix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentier Dessin du Kazé — loop from Saint-Élix&lt;/a&gt; is notable for featuring a sculpture by a Japanese artist representing the wind. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6981873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Land Art Sculpture loop from Le Faubourg&lt;/a&gt;, also highlights local art.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Saint-Élix?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the varied landscape with gentle slopes and river valleys suggests that hiking is enjoyable during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially along shaded river paths.

Q: Can I find easy hikes suitable for beginners in Saint-Élix?
A: Absolutely. There are 6 easy trails around Saint-Élix, perfect for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous walk. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186699893/sentier-dessin-du-kaze-loop-from-saint-elix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentier Dessin du Kazé — loop from Saint-Élix&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent easy option, taking about an hour to complete.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region combines natural beauty with historical heritage. The 'boucle des 3 châteaux' (loop of the 3 castles) is a notable route that allows you to explore historical sites. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6981837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Simorre – Saint Cérase Church loop from Simorre&lt;/a&gt; includes a visit to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3671616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Cérase Church&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Saint-Élix suitable for dogs?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails are not detailed, generally, many trails in natural areas like Saint-Élix are dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or specific trail information before heading out with your pet.
tips:
It is actually a work of art by Japanese artist Suzuki. The work was erected in 2013. The artist was born on March 5, 1956. He graduated from the Tokyo School of Fine Arts. He lives and works in Ermont, France.
The man is known the world over for his "ephemeral" art produced in the great outdoors using natural materials found on site or recovered. He uses them to create his works, bringing out the character of them and their environment. His works have notably been produced in Japan, France, the Netherlands, and Greece.

A tornado… in memory of the Klaus storm of 2009 which devastated 160 trees

The production is called "Kazé" which means "wind" in Japanese. The sculpture of braided branches evokes a cyclone trapped in the forest. Light and majestic, it symbolizes all the strength and power of the wind. It is a memorial work, aiming to remember the terrible Klaus storm of January 24, 2009 which devastated more than 160 trees in this forest of St Elix.
Since the materials are natural, the work gradually degrades over time. Seven years after her erection, she remains in generally stable condition. However, the degradation process will be the reason for this construction in the coming years. So the curious are invited to come and see her soon.

waypoints.name: Kazé - artwork by Japanese artist Teruhisa Suzuki
